Description: Huawei Cloud root account should have MFA enabled. MFA adds an extra layer of protection on top of a username and password. With MFA enabled, when a user signs in to the Huawei Cloud console, they are prompted for their username and password as well as for an authentication code from their MFA device.
Risk: Without MFA on the root account, a compromised password enables full account takeover. An attacker could delete resources, change policies, and disable logging, harming confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

Code.Other:
1. Log on to the Huawei Cloud console as the root user.
2. Choose IAM & Security.
3. Click the Security Settings tab.
4. In the Virtual MFA Device section, click Enable.
5. Scan the QR code with a virtual MFA application (e.g., Google Authenticator).
6. Enter two consecutive verification codes to bind the MFA device.

Recommendation.Text: Enable MFA for the root account and avoid using root credentials for daily operations.
